synthesizedInputs: resolved direction (Mode A — brand-faithful, single variant)
user freeform intent: rooted, irreverent, hand-made. Saturday night and Sunday morning at the same time.
_provenance.scope: target — machine-readable mirror of DESIGN.md + extensions (divergence audit, voice, iaPriorities, narrative)

name: Holler & Hymn
description: Single-maker Appalachian botanical-liqueur house — target visual system

sectionPadding.hardFloorReason: home page has 7 sections; multi-audience hard floor (≤64px / ≥40px) per intent-dimensions.md § 4

quarantined.rule: botanical-context surfaces only (the-still / foraging map / deep-product descriptions)
one-glance accents only — at most one instance per visible viewport; never as body text; never as dominant element on hymnal-black surface

tierAssignments.display: hand-lettered revival-tent — all-caps only, never title case
tierAssignments.body: hymnal serif — reading text only, never at display sizes
tierAssignments.mono: typewriter / IBM Selectric — numerals, dates, ABV, addresses, micro-labels

displayCharacterReferences: hand-painted plywood roadside signs (US-25 north of Asheville)

divergence.mode: Mode A — brand-faithful (signal-strong, no rebrand signal in phrase)

decade.picked_by: anchor-reference: BRAND.md cultural references (1940s revival-tent posters, early-70s outlaw country album sleeves)

craft.picked_by: anchor-reference: BRAND.md imagery direction Lane 3 (label art) + hand-painted roadside signage references

register_flavor.picked_by: brand-native: the holler × hymn duality is the central tension, Memoir-adjacent on hymn side / Tabloid-adjacent on holler side

brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — old-leather-Bible brown-black is the brand's character; pure black is digital, not the register
brand-faithful — decade seed roll (1970s outlaw) could have suggested a brighter rust; captured token preserved
Mode C ground-family override — brand-native two-mode pattern is load-bearing; seed roll yields
brand-faithful — captured face character (revival-tent / state-fair signage) is the brand's distinctive mark

brand_faithful_inversions.retain: hymnal serif body over neutral text serif
brand_faithful_inversions.reason: brand-faithful — old-hymnbook-page register is part of the duality
brand-faithful — typewriter-mono = literalism rule is part of the voice

checked: Editorial-register vocabulary on non-editorial brand (with Hymns-page exception declared)
Fabricated content (no invented stats / awards / customer logos)
Hero text on photographic background without contrast scrim
Generic role names in palette (forbidden, brand-native used)
Centered SaaS hero with dual-CTA pair (forbidden)
antiToolboxAudit.result: clean — all checked, none required removal

site-header.purpose: primary brand nav surface, age-gate clears here

age-gate-modal.constraints: focusable modal not dismissable banner; keyboard-navigable; irreversible no-exit

two-register-section.purpose: the page's scroll rhythm — bulletin / revival alternation
two-register-section.constraint: every section commits to one mode; blending forbidden

find-a-pew-band.purpose: primary CTA band for stockists / visit

hand-painted-card.purpose: per-product card on bottles + home featured
hand-painted-card.constraint: the painted artifact is the imagery; no separate label component

voice.register: plainspoken declaratives; specifics over modifiers; numbers and proper nouns over adjectives
do: Specifics over adjectives — forty miles, 2019, twenty-seven herbs, the preacher's shed.
Sacred/profane wordplay sparingly — lands on product names and label art; restrained in Wendell's narration.
Set numerals in typewriter mono — literalism is the brand's truth-claim.
dont: we / our team / passionate / crafted / curated / purveyors / lifestyle / artisanal / award-winning / finest / unparalleled
adjective stacks of three or more
editorial-register vocabulary outside the Hymns page (atelier, the studio, mise-en-place, the journal, dispatches, the bulletin)

do: Saturday night sin. Sunday morning hymn. Both in one bottle.
Made by hand. From a to z.
Twenty-seven herbs, eleven labels, one preacher's shed.
If you find one in a bar in Asheville, the bartender probably knows me.
I'll pour four spirits. I'll tell you about each one.
dont: Our passionate team of artisans crafts each bottle with care.
An aromatic compound profile of vanilla orchid and crushed velvet.

rules: Voice cheek lives in product names and label art, not in Wendell's narration.
Sacred/profane wordplay is restrained — each instance must land; never every section.
Numbers prove literalism; use them in place of adjectives.
First-person singular everywhere except the legal/utility footer.
Voice 7.2 (no exclamations in chrome) is in effect — exclamation marks are restricted to product labels where they belong (the cheek lives in the labels).

iaPriorities.evidence: home renders two distinct audience CTAs in the first viewport: 'FIND A PEW' (stockist lookup for locals/bartenders) and 'BOOK A SATURDAY TASTING' (visit booking for visitors). Below trigger threshold (>2 audience tracks), but recorded as soft-preserve.

narrative.northStar: Revival-tent poster meets apothecary cabinet drawer — Saturday night sin and Sunday morning hymn at full volume on top of each other. The ampersand is the design.
narrative.overview: Holler & Hymn is a single-maker Appalachian botanical-liqueur house. The visual system has two alternating registers — bulletin mode (cream surface, hymnal-black text) and revival mode (hymnal-black surface, bulletin-cream text) — sharing six brand tokens and three typographic tiers. Mode A inheritance pins palette and type to the captured brand surface; the resolved direction crystallizes the holler × hymn duality at full volume.
keyCharacteristics: Six brand tokens; three (hymnal-black, bulletin-cream, barn-rust) doing most of the work
Two-register alternating sections — same tokens, inverted surfaces
Three-tier type hierarchy: hand-lettered display + hymnal serif body + typewriter mono numerals
All-caps display headlines (title case forbidden for display)
Hand-painted bottle labels as the dominant imagery motif
Hand-imperfection preserved in label art and section ornaments
Flat — no shadows, no gradients, no glassmorphism; painterly imperfection is the only depth
Density: balanced (64px desktop section padding); multi-audience hard floor honored
rules: The Two-Register Alternation Rule: every section commits to bulletin or revival; never blended.
The Hellfire-Neon Restraint Rule: at most one hellfire-neon instance per visible viewport.
The No-Pure-Black Rule: #000000 is forbidden; hymnal-black #1a1410 is the darkest color in the system.
The Botanical-Quarantine Rule: rhody-green #3e5a3e appears only on botanical-context content.
The All-Caps Display Rule: display headlines are always all-caps.
The Numerals-as-Proof Rule: numbers are set in typewriter mono — literalism is the brand's truth-claim.
The Hymnal-Serif-Only-For-Reading Rule: hymnal serif body face never appears at display sizes.
Voice 7.2 (no exclamations in chrome) — exclamations live on product labels only.
IA-priority preservation: audience-routing CTAs stay in the first viewport on home.
dos: Preserve the two-register alternation as the page's scroll rhythm.
Keep hand-painted assets rough — irregular registration, slight paint imperfections, ink density variation.
Restrain hellfire-neon to one glance per viewport.
Use brand-native role names in palette.
Preserve IA priority — audience-routing CTAs stay above the fold on home.
donts: Introduce a gradient, drop shadow, or glassmorphism effect.
Set hand-lettered display in title case.
Invent a sage or beige surface.
Use editorial-register vocabulary (atelier, the studio, mise-en-place, the journal, dispatches, the bulletin) outside the Hymns page.
Fabricate content (invented stats, awards, customer logos, dollar amounts, dates).
Place hero text on photographic background without ≥ 4.5:1 contrast.
